items.briefing:
A newly discovered attack called "GhostPairing" can give criminals unauthorised access to WhatsApp accounts, so staff should be cautious about unexpected pairing requests or linked-device notifications in the app. Separately, Australia has launched a new Office of AI, with experts urging organisations to ensure proper governance around how AI tools are used — a timely reminder as AI adoption continues to outpace oversight policies in many workplaces.

Developers at your organisation should also be aware that targeted malware hidden in open source software packages is increasing significantly, making it important to only download tools from trusted, verified sources.

**Practical tip:** Check your WhatsApp linked devices today (Settings → Linked Devices) and remove any you don't recognise.

featured.title: WhatsApp GhostPairing attack gives intruders access
featured.quote: WhatsApp vulnerability allows persistent account access, threatening millions of Australian users' private messages.
